issues.message: Button is missing an accessible name

evidence.reason: Button has no visible text, aria-label, title, or image alt text.

issues.suggested_fix: Add clear button text or an aria-label that describes the button action.

rule.title: Button missing accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: A button without a name is announced as just 'button' by screen readers, so students cannot tell what pressing it will do.
rule.manual_review_notes: Icon-only buttons may look meaningful visually but still need a programmatic name.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot see names added later by JavaScript or icon fonts.

issues.message: Focused control has no accessible name

evidence.reason: A keyboard user reached this control, but it has no label, text, aria-label, or other usable name.

issues.suggested_fix: Add a visible label, text content, or aria-label so students know what the control does.

rule.title: Focused control has no accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: A keyboard user can reach the control, but without a name a screen reader announces nothing useful about what it does.
rule.manual_review_notes: The accessible name is estimated; confirm with browser dev tools or a screen reader what is actually announced.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Accessible names are estimated from labels, text, and common attributes, not from a full accessibility tree computation.

issues.message: Rendered text may have low contrast

evidence.reason: Computed foreground/background contrast is below the conservative 4.5:1 review threshold.

issues.suggested_fix: Increase foreground/background contrast and confirm the final design with manual review.

rule.title: Rendered text may have low contrast
rule.why_it_matters: Low-vision readers may not be able to read text when the computed foreground and background colors are too similar.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm the sampled colors match what users actually see, especially over gradients, images, and layered components.
rule.browser_check_limitations: The check samples browser-computed colors and does not prove full WCAG color contrast compliance.

issues.message: Fixed or wide content may prevent reflow

evidence.text: Skip to Content Change Text Size or Colors All Translations
evidence.reason: A rendered element is wider than the narrow viewport or uses a large fixed pixel width.

issues.suggested_fix: Replace fixed-width layout with responsive sizing such as max-width: 100%.

rule.title: Fixed or wide content may prevent reflow
rule.why_it_matters: Large fixed-width elements can force horizontal scrolling and hide content when users zoom or use narrow windows.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm whether the wide element is essential and whether all content remains reachable without two-dimensional scrolling.
rule.browser_check_limitations: The check flags obvious wide rendered elements and fixed pixel widths; it may miss complex layout problems.

issues.message: Fixed or wide content may prevent reflow

evidence.text: Skip to Content Change Text Size or Colors All Translations
evidence.reason: A rendered element is wider than the narrow viewport or uses a large fixed pixel width.

issues.suggested_fix: Replace fixed-width layout with responsive sizing such as max-width: 100%.

rule.title: Fixed or wide content may prevent reflow
rule.why_it_matters: Large fixed-width elements can force horizontal scrolling and hide content when users zoom or use narrow windows.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm whether the wide element is essential and whether all content remains reachable without two-dimensional scrolling.
rule.browser_check_limitations: The check flags obvious wide rendered elements and fixed pixel widths; it may miss complex layout problems.

rule.browser_check_limitations: The check flags obvious wide rendered elements and fixed pixel widths; it may miss complex layout problems.

issues.message: Focused element may not show a visible focus indicator

evidence.reason: The focused element did not expose an obvious outline, box-shadow, or border focus style in computed CSS.

issues.suggested_fix: Provide a clear :focus or :focus-visible style with visible outline, border, or shadow.

rule.title: Focused element may not show a visible focus indicator
rule.why_it_matters: Keyboard users with low vision need a visible indicator to know which control currently has focus.
rule.manual_review_notes: Manually tab through the page; some custom focus styles may be visible but not detected by this heuristic.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Focus indicator detection checks computed outline, border, and shadow heuristically and can miss custom visual treatments.

issues.message: Focused element may not show a visible focus indicator

evidence.reason: The focused element did not expose an obvious outline, box-shadow, or border focus style in computed CSS.

issues.suggested_fix: Provide a clear :focus or :focus-visible style with visible outline, border, or shadow.

rule.title: Focused element may not show a visible focus indicator
rule.why_it_matters: Keyboard users with low vision need a visible indicator to know which control currently has focus.
rule.manual_review_notes: Manually tab through the page; some custom focus styles may be visible but not detected by this heuristic.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Focus indicator detection checks computed outline, border, and shadow heuristically and can miss custom visual treatments.

limitations: This prototype runs static HTML checks plus a basic keyboard interaction audit.
It does not replace a full human accessibility audit.
Browser mode does not simulate a full screen reader, and accessible names are estimated.
Low-vision browser checks are conservative heuristics and require manual review.

limitations: Low-vision checks use browser-computed styles and conservative heuristics.
Rendered color contrast checks do not prove full WCAG conformance.
Zoom/reflow uses a narrow viewport approximation rather than a full zoom compliance test.
Focus indicator detection may miss custom focus styles.

ai_scout.summary: Possible accessibility barriers found on the W3C WAI page, including low contrast text and missing button names

ai_suggested_observations.observation: Some text elements may have insufficient contrast with the background, potentially causing readability issues for users with visual impairments
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Low contrast text can make it difficult for users to read and understand the content, potentially excluding them from accessing the information
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: Multiple instances of low contrast text were detected, including 'Accessibility Fundamentals', 'Planning & Policies', and 'Design & Develop'

ai_suggested_observations.observation: A button is missing an accessible name, which could cause issues for users relying on screen readers or other assistive technologies
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Without an accessible name, users may not be able to understand the purpose of the button, potentially preventing them from completing their intended action
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: A button with no visible text, aria-label, title, or image alt text was detected

ai_suggested_observations.observation: A focused control has no accessible name, which could cause issues for users relying on keyboard navigation
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Without an accessible name, users may not be able to understand the purpose of the control, potentially preventing them from completing their intended action
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: A focused control with no label, text, aria-label, or other usable name was detected at step 9

outreach_notes: We would appreciate the opportunity to discuss these potential accessibility barriers with your team and explore ways to improve the overall accessibility of the W3C WAI page
limitations: AI Scout findings are suggestions and need human review.
AI Scout output is not a confirmed accessibility finding.
AI Scout does not determine legal compliance, WCAG compliance, or screen-reader behavior.
AI Scout findings are suggestions and need human review
AI Scout output is not a needs human review accessibility finding
AI Scout does not determine legal compliance, WCAG compliance, or screen-reader behavior
